system.description: Named for le Comte de la Fère, it is a style not invented by him but much associated with his employment of it. The style superficially resembles the French or Toreador Styles, employing an epee in one hand and a cape in the off-hand. However, it is an aggressive style, using the cape as though it were a weapon, often concealing a crushing fist.

finalSecret.description: When you strike an enemy with a Brawling attack, you get a Bonus Die to hit and damage with Bladework attacks against that enemy until the end of your next turn. This effect ends if you Yield Advantage in the meantime.
finalSecret.name: Relentless Attacker

benefit.name: The Iron Fist in the Velvet Cloak
benefit.description: You may use a cloak to Beat and Disarm, and you may also use it to Barehand Parry (as well as Cloak Parry). The cloak-covered hand has Protection of 1d2 if not protected by a heavy glove. When you get a Mighty Success using the cloak, you may make a free Brawling attack. You get +1 to any rolls to Goad an enemy while wielding a cloak.
